knownFor: American voice actor.
reveal: Between the prequel films, the animated Anakin of Genndy Tartakovsky's 2003 Clone Wars micro-series was voiced by Mat Lucas, standing in as Hayden Christensen's audio double across all 25 chapters.

production: Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (NPR radio drama)
knownFor: American stage and TV actor (Bridget Loves Bernie, St. Elsewhere) and prolific audiobook narrator.
reveal: In NPR's 1996 Return of the Jedi radio drama, the redeemed, unmasked Anakin Skywalker who speaks to Luke in his dying breath was voiced by David Birney.

production: Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (NPR radio drama)
knownFor: American stage and screen actor (The Winds of War, Sisters).
reveal: Jabba's oily Twi'lek majordomo Bib Fortuna finally got a voice in NPR's 1996 Return of the Jedi radio drama, supplied by veteran actor David Dukes.

knownFor: Canadian actor, jazz musician and voice artist (Sabretooth in X-Men, Mok Swagger in Rock & Rule).
reveal: Boba Fett spoke his very first words not in a film but in the animated segment of 1978's much-maligned Star Wars Holiday Special, and the debut voice of the galaxy's most iconic bounty hunter was Canadian actor Don Francks.

knownFor: Voice actress, ADR director and singer (Major Motoko Kusanagi in Ghost in the Shell: Stand Alone Complex).
reveal: The icy Imperial Governor Arihnda Pryce who menaced the Ghost crew through two seasons of Star Wars Rebels was voiced by Mary Elizabeth McGlynn, better known as the anime-dub voice of Major Kusanagi.

knownFor: Actor known as Andre 'Junior' Johnson Jr. on black-ish.
reveal: The young High Republic Jedi Bell Zettifar in the animated preschool series Young Jedi Adventures is voiced by Marcus Scribner, Junior from black-ish.

knownFor: Teen actress from Avengers: Infinity War (young Gamora) and Barbie.
reveal: Under the orange skin, white face-markings and towering montrals of the young Padawan Ahsoka in the flashback episode 'Shadow Warrior' is Ariana Greenblatt, the same teenager who played young Gamora and Sasha in Barbie.

portrait.author: Gage Skidmore from Surprise, AZ, United States of America

knownFor: German actor known internationally as Dukhat in Babylon 5 and Esoqq in Star Trek: The Next Generation.
reveal: Behind the Minbari bone-crest of Dukhat, Delenn's revered mentor, is German actor Reiner Schöne, who also became the German dubbing voice of Darth Vader after Heinz Petruo's death, which is how his name surfaced in a Star Wars census.

knownFor: American painter and character actress who physically portrayed the Emperor's hologram in the original theatrical cut of The Empire Strikes Back.
reveal: The Emperor who first menaced Vader was a 78-year-old woman: painter-actress Marjorie Eaton, buried under makeup with chimpanzee eyes optically superimposed and a man's voice dubbed over her. She went uncredited and was quietly replaced by Ian McDiarmid in the 2004 re-release.

knownFor: American character actor who was the original, long-uncredited voice of bounty hunter Boba Fett in The Empire Strikes Back.
reveal: The menacing drawl of the original Boba Fett belonged to Jason Wingreen, better known as Harry the bartender on All in the Family. He went uncredited for two decades before his lines were re-dubbed by Temuera Morrison in 2004.

knownFor: New Zealand stage and screen actor who supplied the voice of the Emperor's hologram in the original theatrical cut of The Empire Strikes Back.
reveal: Before Ian McDiarmid ever donned the robes, the Emperor's rasping hologram was voiced by veteran actor Clive Revill, dubbed over a silent stand-in and scrubbed from the film in the 2004 re-release.

knownFor: British-American actor who voiced Chancellor Palpatine and Darth Sidious across the animated Star Wars: The Clone Wars until his death.
reveal: The silky menace of the animated Sidious was Ian Abercrombie, the same actor audiences knew as Seinfeld's fussy Mr. Pitt, voicing the Sith Lord right up until he died in 2012.

knownFor: American actor who voiced the young rebel Ezra Bridger across all four seasons of the animated Star Wars Rebels.
reveal: The Force-sensitive street kid Ezra Bridger was given voice by Taylor Gray, a live-action TV actor who never once appears on screen in the series he anchors.

knownFor: American actor who played Rebel gunner Dak Ralter and doubled inside the Boba Fett armor for the Cloud City scenes of The Empire Strikes Back.
reveal: When Jeremy Bulloch was pulled away to play an Imperial officer, the Boba Fett confronting Vader in the Bespin corridor was actually John Morton, the same man seen unmasked moments earlier as Luke's doomed snowspeeder gunner, Dak.

knownFor: Brazilian-American capoeira martial artist and Emmy-winning stunt performer who fights inside the beskar armor whenever Din Djarin throws a punch.
reveal: Every acrobatic, armor-clad brawl in The Mandalorian is Lateef Crowder dos Santos under the helmet, not Pedro Pascal, a capoeira stuntman who won a Primetime Emmy for a role in which his face is never once seen.

knownFor: Prolific American voice actor, familiar to gamers as Victor "Sully" Sullivan across the Uncharted series.
reveal: The wheezing four-armed cyborg general of the 2D Clone Wars micro-series is voiced by Richard McGonagle — the same warm gravel behind Nathan Drake's cigar-chomping mentor Sully.

knownFor: The original voice of accident-prone pilot Launchpad McQuack on DuckTales and Darkwing Duck.
reveal: The Mon Calamari admiral barking orders in Rogue Squadron is Terry McGovern — voice of Launchpad McQuack, and the man whose ad-libbed "I think I ran over a wookiee back there" on THX 1138 handed George Lucas the word.

production: Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader
knownFor: Veteran LucasArts video game composer known for Grim Fandango and the Sly Cooper series.
reveal: The fish-faced admiral in Rogue Leader is voiced not by a career actor but by Peter McConnell — one of LucasArts's own composers, stepping out from behind the score to voice Ackbar.

knownFor: American voice actor also heard as Rafael Diaz in Star vs. the Forces of Evil and Shang Tsung in the Mortal Kombat Legends films.
reveal: The Clone Wars-era Ackbar is voiced by Artt Butler, who began not in the booth but at a reception desk running a talent agency before slipping under the Mon Calamari's voice himself.

production: Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (radio drama)
knownFor: Seven-time Emmy winner beloved as gruff newsman Lou Grant and later as Carl Fredricksen in Pixar's Up.
reveal: The bloated Huttese crime lord of NPR's 1996 Return of the Jedi is Ed Asner — Lou Grant himself — who insisted on grunting Jabba's alien dialogue with no electronic effects, puffing up "like a bullfrog" at the microphone.

knownFor: Canadian voice actress famous as Princess Celestia in My Little Pony: Friendship Is Magic.
reveal: The green Twi'lek rebel pilot in the LEGO Droid Tales specials is voiced by Nicole Oliver — better known to a generation as the regal Princess Celestia of Equestria.

portrait.author: Super Festivals from Ft. Lauderdale, USA

knownFor: American actress known for Scott Pilgrim vs. the World, 10 Cloverfield Lane, and Birds of Prey.
reveal: Hera's live-action debut hides Mary Elizabeth Winstead beneath emerald skin and a pair of prosthetic lekku head-tails, trading her own features for the Twi'lek general's.

production: Star Wars: Jedi Knight – Mysteries of the Sith
knownFor: Canadian voice actor best known as Batman in the animated series The Batman and as an early Spider-Man.
reveal: The gruff ex-Imperial mercenary Kyle Katarn is Rino Romano — the same throat that would later become the Dark Knight himself in The Batman.

production: Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader
knownFor: Tony-nominated stage actor (Dreamgirls) and a prolific television and voice performer.
reveal: Lando's smooth patter across the Rogue Squadron games is Broadway veteran Obba Babatundé, voice-matching Billy Dee Williams so closely most players never noticed the swap.

knownFor: Ubiquitous game voice actor best known as Lee Everett in Telltale's The Walking Dead.
reveal: Years before he broke a generation's heart as Lee Everett, Dave Fennoy was already Lando — calmly running the Rebel fleet's comms in X-Wing Alliance.

production: Return of the Jedi (NPR radio drama)
knownFor: Screen actor best known as Adam Green on the sitcom Ellen.
reveal: On NPR's dramatized Return of the Jedi, the voice gambling for the galaxy as Lando Calrissian is Arye Gross — Ellen DeGeneres's sitcom roommate.

portrait.author: Arye Gross pour le domaine public

knownFor: Award-winning voice actor known as Hawks in My Hero Academia and Hunter in The Owl House.
reveal: The young Lando of Lego Star Wars: All-Stars is Zeno Robinson, voice-matching Donald Glover years before anime fans crowned him Hawks.

portrait.author: John Manard for Super Festivals from Ft. Lauderdale, USA

production: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
knownFor: Experimental musician and comedian, longtime bandleader on The Late Late Show with James Corden.
reveal: The muffled voice of the disguised Lando in The Rise of Skywalker isn't Billy Dee Williams — it's Reggie Watts, whom J.J. Abrams pulled off his couch mid-video-game to dub the lines.

portrait.author: Gage Skidmore from Peoria, AZ, United States of America

production: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
knownFor: Norwegian actress who served as the on-set body and face performance for the film's final shot.
reveal: The 19-year-old Leia who turns to the camera at the end of Rogue One is Norwegian actress Ingvild Deila — her own face digitally painted over with a CGI resurrection of Carrie Fisher.

knownFor: Voice actress best known as Duchess Satine Kryze in Star Wars: The Clone Wars.
reveal: Leia in Disney Infinity is Anna Graves — the same voice that gave Mandalore's pacifist duchess Satine her spine, now channeling Carrie Fisher.

knownFor: Actress known as Diane Miller on General Hospital and for roles on Cougar Town and True Blood.
reveal: General Leia's voice in Star Wars Resistance is soap-opera fixture Carolyn Hennesy, who stepped into the role and carried the general through the series' final stretch.

knownFor: Voice actress and professional stage magician, married to writer Paul Dini.
reveal: The Leia barking orders across the Battlefront reboots is Misty Lee — a working magician offscreen, this time making the princess vanish into pure audio.

knownFor: Actress and voice actress seen in American Horror Story and The Social Network.
reveal: Across Forces of Destiny and the Lego specials, teenage Leia is Shelby Young — cast under a code name for how close she could land to a young Carrie Fisher.

knownFor: Versatile voice and screen actor who voices Appa and Momo in Netflix's Avatar: The Last Airbender.
reveal: The High Republic Jedi Master Loden Greatstorm is Matthew Yang King — a character actor who elsewhere speaks only in the grunts of Avatar's flying bison.

production: Star Wars: Return of the Jedi
knownFor: American radio actress whose gravelly voice became E.T. in Steven Spielberg's E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial.
reveal: When Leia infiltrates Jabba's palace disguised as the bounty hunter Boushh, the electronic Ubese growls are Pat Welsh — the two-pack-a-day voice that, one year earlier, had been E.T.

knownFor: Voice actress who played Kanga across Disney's Winnie the Pooh productions.
reveal: For two decades of Star Wars story-time records, the voice of Princess Leia belonged to Patricia Parris — better known for mothering Roo as Kanga in the Hundred Acre Wood.

knownFor: Actress and producer, Robot Chicken regular and co-founder of Team Unicorn, married to Seth Green.
reveal: The whip-slinging bounty hunter Latts Razzi in The Clone Wars is Clare Grant — Seth Green's wife, disappearing behind an alien mercenary rather than her own face.

portrait.author: Photo by Eric Blackmon posted to Clare Grant's personal Flickr page.

production: Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace
knownFor: British comedian and actor known for Spaced, Guardians of the Galaxy, and The Tick.
reveal: Ray Park's snarling Sith was silent on set — the growling voice of Darth Maul was dubbed over the entire performance by comedian Peter Serafinowicz.

production: Star Wars: Episode II – Attack of the Clones
knownFor: American filmmaker best known for the low-budget Star Wars parody short Hardware Wars.
reveal: The man who spoofed Star Wars with Hardware Wars ended up inside it — sound designer Ben Burtt recorded Fosselius's voice and played it backwards to build the Geonosian chittering of Poggle the Lesser.

production: Lego Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga
knownFor: American-British actor and voice artist known for voicing Malos in Xenoblade Chronicles 2 and Breach in Valorant.
reveal: Kept secret for months, the voice of Luke Skywalker throughout Lego Star Wars: The Skywalker Saga is David Menkin, not Mark Hamill.

knownFor: Prolific American voice actor known for Kittan in Gurren Lagann and the Joker in the Lego Batman games.
reveal: In Disney's Phineas and Ferb: Star Wars crossover, Luke Skywalker is voiced by Christopher Corey Smith — reportedly the only actor besides Mark Hamill to play both Luke and the Joker.

production: Lego Star Wars: The Yoda Chronicles
knownFor: Canadian actor known for Philip Banks in Bel-Air and Robert April in Star Trek: Strange New Worlds.
reveal: Bel-Air's Philip Banks stepped in for Samuel L. Jackson — Adrian Holmes voiced Jedi Master Mace Windu across the animated Lego Star Wars specials.

production: Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (NPR radio drama)
knownFor: American actress known for Admiral Alynna Nechayev in Star Trek: The Next Generation and Deep Space Nine.
reveal: Star Trek's stern Admiral Nechayev crossed the streams — Natalia Nogulich voiced Rebel leader Mon Mothma in NPR's Return of the Jedi radio dramatization.

knownFor: Canadian-based character actor and two-time Gemini Award winner.
reveal: When NPR dramatized the original trilogy, Obi-Wan Kenobi passed from Alec Guinness to veteran character actor Bernard Behrens — heard across all three broadcasts but never seen.

knownFor: British actor known for Game of Thrones' Wisdom Hallyne and Beauty and the Beast, and a Guinness record for most character voices in an audiobook.
reveal: The prolific British voice-over veteran Roy Dotrice lent his gravitas to Obi-Wan Kenobi in the official Star Wars read-along storybook recordings.

production: Star Wars Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike
knownFor: American actor known for Carlton Lassiter in Psych and King Richard in Galavant.
reveal: Before Psych's Detective Lassiter, Timothy Omundson voiced Obi-Wan Kenobi in the GameCube's Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike.

production: Star Wars Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike
knownFor: American voice actor best known as Krang and Casey Jones in the 1987 Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles.
reveal: TMNT's brain-alien Krang, Pat Fraley, turns up as Luke's Uncle Owen Lars in the video game Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike.

knownFor: New Zealand actress known for Fear the Walking Dead: Flight 462 and Neighbours.
reveal: The young clone Omega, heart of The Bad Batch, is voiced by New Zealander Michelle Ang — who kept her native accent as a nod to Temuera Morrison's Jango Fett.

knownFor: American comedian and actress known for her stand-up, The New Adventures of Old Christine, and Black-ish.
reveal: The swashbuckling space pirate Phee Genoa in The Bad Batch is comedian Wanda Sykes, unrecognizable behind the animated rogue.

production: Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace
knownFor: Playing the horn-skulled Iktotchi Jedi Master Saesee Tiin on the Jedi High Council.
reveal: Behind the downward-curling horns and leathery alien face was English actor and martial artist Khan Bonfils, who died in 2015 aged just 42.

knownFor: Carrying the blue-skinned Chiss strategist Grand Admiral Thrawn from animation into live action.
reveal: Roughly three hours of blue prosthetics, red contact lenses and a slicked-back wig transform Lars Mikkelsen — brother of Mads — into Thrawn, the villain he had only voiced in Star Wars Rebels.

knownFor: Voicing the Mandalorian graffiti artist and explosives expert Sabine Wren for four seasons of Star Wars Rebels.
reveal: The colour-splashing teen Mando is voiced by American actress Tiya Sircar, seen on camera as the fake Eleanor in The Good Place.

portrait.author: Super Festivals from Ft. Lauderdale, USA

knownFor: Originating the voice of militant rebel leader Saw Gerrera in Star Wars: The Clone Wars.
reveal: The gravel-voiced extremist — later given a face by Forest Whitaker — was first voiced by Canadian actor and rapper Andrew Kishino.

knownFor: Voicing the cartwheeling, chi-blocking acrobat Ty Lee in Avatar: The Last Airbender.
reveal: The bubbly circus performer Ty Lee is voiced by Olivia Hack — the former child Cindy Brady who also gives voice to Star Wars' Qi'ra in Forces of Destiny.

knownFor: Playing the green-skinned Mirialan Jedi Master Vernestra Rwoh in The Acolyte.
reveal: Beneath the sallow-green Mirialan skin and diamond facial markings is Canadian actress Rebecca Henderson — Lizzy from Russian Doll and wife of the show's creator Leslye Headland.

knownFor: English actor known for Peter Quinn in Homeland and the title role in Hitman: Agent 47.
reveal: Under four hours of Pau'an prosthetics and a 45-pound suit — only his mouth and nose left exposed — is Rupert Friend, sweating through takes to give the Grand Inquisitor its silky menace in live action.

knownFor: English actor best known as Lucius Malfoy in the Harry Potter films.
reveal: The reptilian purr of Rebels' first Inquisitor is Jason Isaacs — an award-winning vocal turn, no wand and no face, that he reprised a decade later in Tales of the Empire.

production: Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi
knownFor: American actress and director known for the Jurassic World films and for directing episodes of The Mandalorian.
reveal: Yaddle sat silent through The Phantom Menace; when the tiny Yoda-species Jedi finally spoke in Tales of the Jedi, the voice belonged to Bryce Dallas Howard.

production: Star Wars: Episode I – The Phantom Menace
knownFor: English puppeteer who joined the Jim Henson Creature Shop in 1986 and performed in films from Labyrinth to The Muppet Christmas Carol.
reveal: Frank Oz gets the credit, but the Phantom Menace Yoda puppet took three sets of hands — one of them Don Austen's, hand-picked by Oz to help work the Jedi Master.

knownFor: English puppeteer, ventriloquist and writer — creator and star of the BBC children's series Bodger & Badger.
reveal: The wrinkled, lumbering Ephant Mon lurking in Jabba's palace was mimed and puppeteered by Andy Cunningham — better known to British kids as Simon Bodger of Bodger & Badger.

knownFor: American voice actor, comedian and impressionist who has impersonated celebrities on The Howard Stern Show and MADtv.
reveal: Yoda's backwards wisdom in Young Jedi Adventures comes from Piotr Michael, a Groundlings-trained impressionist stepping into the sandals Frank Oz wore for over forty years.

knownFor: American writer, puppeteer and voice actor, and a co-creator of the Fox sitcom Greg the Bunny.
reveal: The crude, wisecracking puppet Greg the Bunny is co-creator Dan Milano's own hand and voice — the same Milano who sneaked into Star Wars voicing Jedi Master Yarael Poof for Robot Chicken.

knownFor: Canadian actor, screenwriter and producer who also voiced Tenderheart Bear and Bright Heart Raccoon in the Care Bears franchise.
reveal: Warwick Davis wore the Ewok on film; when Wicket got his own Saturday-morning cartoon, the plucky voice for season one was Canadian actor Jim Henshaw.

knownFor: American voice actor — Murray the demonic skull in the Monkey Island games and the deadpan 'Got Milk?' tagline.
reveal: For the Ewoks cartoon's second season Wicket was voiced by Denny Delk, the same throat behind Monkey Island's Murray and a decade of 'Got Milk?' ads.

knownFor: A 1990s British R&B star turned Star Wars alien performer, credited under creatures across the sequel trilogy and anthology films.
reveal: Inside Slowen Lo, the busybody Abednedo who snitches on Finn and Rose at the Canto Bight casino, is Dee Tails, once a chart-topping member of the boy band MN8. The alien's grumbling voice was dubbed over him by Joseph Gordon-Levitt.

knownFor: Guillermo del Toro's hand-crafted, marble-pale interpretation of Frankenstein's abandoned creation.
reveal: Jacob Elordi performed beneath 42 separate prosthetic appliances devised around his body and movement. Mike Hill's design preserves the Creature's vulnerability inside a face assembled from the dead.

references.label: Elordi's Creature performance and 42-piece prosthetic build

knownFor: The humanoid architect of the Upside Down's terror in the fourth and fifth seasons.
reveal: Jamie Campbell Bower played Vecna physically rather than yielding the entire character to animation. Barrie Gower's interlocking appliances turn his head and body into exposed flesh, roots and vines.

references.label: Behind the scenes of Bower becoming Vecna

knownFor: The schoolboy at the center of a family drama about living visibly with Treacher Collins syndrome.
reveal: Jacob Tremblay was nine when he carried the film beneath a full prosthetic transformation. Arjen Tuiten built the neck, cheeks, chin, teeth and contact-lens work while treating Auggie as a child rather than an effect.

references.label: Arjen Tuiten on Tremblay's Auggie prosthetics

production: The Chronicles of Narnia: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
designer: Howard Berger · Tami Lane · Sarah Rubano
knownFor: The nervous faun whose meeting with Lucy first gives Narnia a human heart.
reveal: James McAvoy performed through a practical nose, forehead, ears, horns, wig and hand-laid hair. Howard Berger, Tami Lane and Sarah Rubano built the upper-body transformation while visual effects completed the goat legs.

references.label: McAvoy identifies the artists who built Mr. Tumnus

knownFor: Universal's modern return to the doomed aristocrat and the werewolf inside him.
reveal: Benicio del Toro carries Lawrence Talbot into Rick Baker and Dave Elsey's practical werewolf transformation. The makeup deliberately echoes Jack Pierce's human-readable 1941 Wolf Man.

references.label: The Wolfman makeup design and Academy Award credits

designer: Barney Burman · Mindy Hall · Joel Harlow
knownFor: The tattooed Romulan captain whose revenge creates the Kelvin timeline.
reveal: Eric Bana shaved his head and entered the film beneath redesigned Romulan prosthetics and dense ritual scarring. The makeup team converts a familiar leading man into the furious miner commanding the Narada.

references.label: Nero's character and Bana's performance
Barney Burman on the Star Trek makeup team

knownFor: The pale Dark Elf ruler seeking to return the universe to primordial darkness.
reveal: Christopher Eccleston performed through David White's thin silicone Dark Elf appliances and later battle damage. The transformation masks the former Doctor inside an austere, reptilian face.

references.label: Production notes assign Malekith's makeup to David White

knownFor: The blue Ravager captain who abducted Peter Quill and became his rough surrogate father.
reveal: Michael Rooker sits underneath Yondu's blue skin, teeth, contact lenses and cranial fin. David White and Elizabeth Yianni-Georgiou's alien treatment lets Rooker's weathered expressions survive the color and appliances.

references.label: Building the practical characters of Guardians of the Galaxy
David White discusses Yondu's makeup

knownFor: Robert Eggers's decayed Transylvanian nobleman, built as a folkloric corpse rather than a conventional screen vampire.
reveal: Bill Skarsgård disappears beneath David White's extensive facial and body prosthetics. The familiar Pennywise performer supplies Orlok's posture and voice from inside an entirely different monster.

references.label: David White on creating Skarsgård's Count Orlok

portrait.author: Gage Skidmore from Peoria, AZ, United States of America

knownFor: The leonine, poetry-loving guardian of the hidden community beneath New York.
reveal: Ron Perlman found his breakthrough beneath Rick Baker's feline brow, muzzle, teeth and hair. The makeup conceals the actor while leaving enough movement for Vincent to anchor a weekly romantic drama.

references.label: Perlman on performing Vincent beneath the makeup
Series history credits Rick Baker with Vincent's makeup

production: Hellboy / Hellboy II: The Golden Army
knownFor: The elegant amphibious psychic and indispensable agent of the B.P.R.D.
reveal: Doug Jones supplies Abe's complete physical performance beneath Spectral Motion's aquatic prosthetics and bodysuit. Mike Elizalde's team rebuilt the shoulder and chest forms around Jones and animated the character's delicate gills.

references.label: Doug Jones discusses performing Abe Sapien
Mike Elizalde discusses creating Abe Sapien

knownFor: Mike Ehrmantraut in Breaking Bad and Better Call Saul — the same watchful stillness, buried here beneath an immortal soldier's battered alien face.
reveal: Jonathan Banks played Golin Shel-la, the exhausted Ennis leader condemned to an endless war in DS9's “Battle Lines.” Production testimony singles out both Banks's performance and the makeup that makes the future Mike Ehrmantraut easy to miss.

references.label: Golin Shel-la character page credits Jonathan Banks
Episode production notes describe Banks wearing the character makeup
Westmore served as Star Trek makeup designer and supervisor
Jonathan Banks biography and credited roles

portrait.author:
Netta_Bank_and_Jonathan_Banks.jpg: Netta Bank
derivative work: RanZag (talk)

knownFor: Hector Salamanca in Breaking Bad and Better Call Saul — years before the bell, Margolis was a Tanugan scientist reconstructed from competing testimony.
reveal: Mark Margolis played Dr. Nel Apgar beneath the enlarged forehead and repeated brow ridges of a Tanugan in “A Matter of Perspective.” Every appearance is itself a holographic reconstruction, making the designed face part of an episode already obsessed with unreliable recognition.

references.label: Nel Apgar character page credits Mark Margolis and identifies him as Tanugan
Tanugan reference identifies the species' designed forehead and brow ridges
Westmore served as Star Trek makeup designer and supervisor
Mark Margolis biography and credited roles
